hero.tagline: The goal of our work is to centre the human experiences and values to improve equity and thoughtful, meaningful, and inclusive technology use in healthcare.
hero.land_acknowledgement: Racialized settler on the unceded and traditional territories of the Syilx Okanagan

director.name: Charlene E. Ronquillo
director.title: Assistant Professor
director.department: School of Nursing, Faculty of Health and Social Development
director.address:
ART360 (Arts Building)

Kelowna, BC Canada V1V 1V7
bio: The HIE Lab’s work is interdisciplinary and underpinned by critical theories and implementation science. A key aim in our research is to ensure meaningful inclusion of non-dominant groups in the conceptualization, design, development, and implementation of health technologies in health systems.

Charlene has expertise in mixed methods, participatory, and co-production approaches, HCI, and employing critical perspectives throughout the technology lifecycle. Charlene has experience with various aspects of health information technology in health systems and nursing, including nursing informatics competencies, usability research, person-centred design and development, rapid and exploratory prototyping, and technology adoption and usage.

director.funding_acknowledgement: My work is/has been funded by AMS Healthcare, Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada, Canadian Nurses Foundation, the Brocher Foundation, Roche Canada, UBC Health Innovation Funding Investment (HIFI), UBC Okanagan Multidisciplinary Undergraduate Research Projects in Health (MURPH), UBC SoTL, Ryerson FCS Covid-19 Rapid Response Grant, UBC School of Nursing Funds.

active.title: Mapping trust in nurses with dimensions of trustworthy artificial intelligence: A scoping review
active.co_investigators: Booth, R., Janke, R., Chan, R., Recsky, C.
active.description: Is it possible for artificial intelligence (AI) models to demonstrate trustworthiness in ways that the world’s consistently most trusted professionals – nurses – are thought to do? Or is it that some dimensions of trustworthiness are only demonstrable by humans? This scoping review and thematic analysis will map and synthesize the evidence on nursing trust and understand parallels to computational understandings of trustworthiness, in order to envision novel approaches to developing trustworthy AI.

active.title: Identifying patient-validated compassion and equity concepts in community care clinical notes through natural language processing
active.description: In this project, we build on the Health Equity Documentation study to develop and test compassion and equity-grounded natural language processing (NLP) application to leverage otherwise unused community nursing clinical notes.

active.title: Thinking Aloud with AI: Nursing Students’ Cognitive Processes Using GenAI for Clinical Reasoning
active.co_investigators: Wilson, R., Willson, M., Garcia Chua, R., Moralejo, L., Kennedy L., Sturgess, K.
active.description: This project explores how undergraduate nursing students use generative AI tools like ChatGPT while working on complex clinical tasks, such as creating care plans or responding to patient emergencies.

active.title: Stakeholder perspectives and impacts of explainable artificial intelligence: A case study in a British Columbia health authority
active.funding: SSHRC Insight Development Grant (2023–2025); UBC HIFI Awards (2022–23)
active.co_investigators: Conati, C., Currie, L.M., Hamarneh, G., Janke, R., Li, X., Block, L., Beschastnikh, I.
active.description: This study employs realist methods to explore considerations for health systems operations and clinician end-user implications of developing and implementing explainable AI and clinical decision support tools.

active.title: Pre-processing community nursing and allied health data for equity-informed artificial intelligence for interdisciplinary wound care
active.description: Health equity information about patients are often captured in narrative clinical notes by nurses and allied health professionals in electronic health records. This project aims to complete the resource-intensive data pre-processing step to enable the use of data from two electronic health records to develop equity-informed AI applications.

active.title: Capturing health equity in community nursing electronic documentation
active.description: In this project, we aim to understand community health nurses’ conceptualizations and documentation of health equity in electronic health records to establish the foundation for future work using natural language processing to build machine learning predictive risk models.

team.values: Above all, we value doing good work with good people.
team.description: We work as a collaborative and value the unique and diverse contributions each person brings. We work on interesting problems to help technology be used for good. We are interested in developing thoughtful and inclusive technologies that are meaningful and useful to people.

current.name: Dr. Chantelle Recsky
current.bio: Dr. Recsky is a Registered Nurse and her areas of interest include health information technologies, quality and safety, and knowledge translation, as well as supporting students to gain knowledge and experience in research.

current.name: Nicole Ketter
current.bio: Nicole earned her MSc in Rehabilitation Science from UBC, beginning in the lab as a work-study student in 2022 before stepping into the role of research manager in 2023. While her thesis focused on chronic disease multimorbidity prevention and management, she is particularly drawn to the dynamic intersection of technology and health research explored in the HIE lab. Nicole enjoys mentoring students and contributing to the lab’s supportive, team-oriented culture. Outside the lab, she loves hiking and swimming with her dog, Ollie.

current.name: Ruinan Jin
current.bio: Ruinan is currently a graduate student in Electrical and Computer Engineering at UBC. He previously earned a BSc in Computer Science, during which he spent over two years working at the HIE lab. His research centres on wound infection prediction and health equity in this process, and he is especially interested in exploring cutting-edge machine learning engineering techniques.

current.name: Andrea Burrows
current.bio: Andrea is a Registered Nurse and Graduate Student working on her Ph.D. at UBCO in health informatics. She is a mother of two curious and active boys. In parallel with her studies, Andrea works for the Interior Health Research Department as a Manager of Research to create and foster linkages between health system actors and community partners.

current.name: Naureen Mukhi
current.bio: Naureen is a Registered Nurse and Graduate Research Assistant working on her Masters in Nursing at UBC Okanagan. Her thesis is on investigating health system stakeholders’ perspectives and use of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for clinical decision support tools. Naureen has recently completed her diploma in Digital Health and Data Analytics from the Michener Institute of Education in Ontario.

current.name: Josephine Nanyonjo
current.bio: Josephine is deeply committed to addressing the persistent health inequities that exist globally. As a Registered Nurse, Josephine has dedicated the past few years to improving healthcare in rural and underserved areas of Uganda, utilising her knowledge, skills, and time to make a tangible impact. Josephine is particularly enthusiastic about the potential of using technology to provide innovative solutions to this enduring global challenge.

current.name: Jianan Wang
current.bio: Jianan is a nursing PhD student at Zhejiang University, China, and is currently a visiting international research student at UBCO. She received a China Scholarship Council (CSC) Scholarship (2025–26) to support her time at UBCO. Her research focuses on the ethical implications of information technology in the nursing field.

current.name: Kate Sturgess
current.bio: Kate is a current nursing student at UBCO and originally from Calgary, Alberta. Kate is hoping to pursue a master’s in nursing specialising in research. She grew up ski racing and loves to spend time in the mountains between classes.

current.name: Vanessa Lo
current.bio: Vanessa is a Registered Nurse passionate about medical device development and integrating digital technologies into healthcare. She is also interested in working with vulnerable populations to minimise health disparities so that more equitable care can be provided to all members of the community.

current.name: Winnifred Adzo Vittor
current.bio: Winnifred is a Registered Nurse and a prospective MSN student from Ghana with interest in nursing informatics. She looks forward to learning how technology can be integrated into health, especially nursing.

current.name: Amrit Kaur Dhami
current.bio: Amrit is currently in their third year of nursing and thrilled to be a part of the research team. She is deeply committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive community within the team. She believes that research provides an excellent opportunity to contribute to the enhancement of nursing experiences.

current.name: Abubakari Mohammed Sherif
current.bio: Abubakari Mohammed Sherif is a Registered Nurse from Ghana with an interest in improving electronic health records in rural communities in Ghana. He is a prospective graduate student.

current.name: Jannatul Ferdous Shapnil
current.bio: Jannatul is a registered doctor in Bangladesh under the Bangladesh Medical and Dental Council. Their primary research focus is equitable healthcare and health informatics. Jannatul is also a cat parent to Kai and likes watching cat memes.

news.text: We’re excited to welcome Dr. Hilal Yildirim from Inonu University (Turkey) as a visiting scientist for one year.
Charlene was elected President-Elect of the Canadian Nursing Informatics Association (CNIA).
The lab launched the Thinking Aloud with AI study, exploring how nursing students use generative AI for clinical reasoning, supported by a UBC SoTL Seed Grant.
New publication in Journal of Nursing Scholarship: The Overlooked Dark Side of Generative AI in Nursing.
Congratulations to Amrit and Kate for their fantastic presentation at WRCASN titled, “Student Research Experiences in the Integration of AI Technologies and Its Relevance to Nursing Education.”
We’re excited to welcome Dr. Clarissa Bohrer da Silva as a visiting postdoc. She joins us from the Universidade do Estado de Santa Catarina in Chapecó, Brazil.
Naureen is awarded a SSHRC CGS-M Award for her Masters thesis work. Congratulations!
Andrea completes a major program milestone and achieves PhD Candidacy. Congratulations!
Charlene and the NAIL Collaborative host the Embedding nursing expertise in artificial intelligence-based health technology development workshop at Fondation Brocher in Hermance, Switzerland.
Charlene gave a keynote at the 2024 Health Research and AI: A CMDO Symposium in Montreal, QC.
Charlene gave her first international keynote and presented a paper and 2 posters at NI2024 in Manchester, UK.
Charlene and members of the NAIL Collaborative were invited as experts to the “Artificial Intelligence in Reality (AIR) Roundtable for Nursing and Midwifery,” organized by NHS England.
Charlene is Co-I on a newly funded UBCO Eminence Research Cluster: Data Safety and AI Literacy (2024–27).
Two new publications: Evaluating the Representation of Disaster Hazards in SNOMED CT (JAMIA) and Differing Effects of Implementation Leadership on Nurses’ Use of mHealth (JMIR Nursing).
Charlene and the NAIL Collaborative are awarded Artificial Intelligence Journal (Elsevier) funding for the workshop on Ethical, Social, and Legal Implications of Using Generative AI in Healthcare.
Sharon, Nicole, and Charlene give 5 presentations at CNIA 2023 (Calgary, AB) and at the International Council of Nurses Congress 2023 (Montreal, QC).
Charlene and Dr. Xiaoxiao Li are awarded the UBC Collaborative Research Mobility Award to support collaboration across the UBC Vancouver and Okanagan campuses.
Our team is awarded a Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC) Insight Development Grant.
Charlene receives the 2023 UBC School of Nursing Recent Alumni Award.
Charlene and the NAIL Collaborative receive support from Fondation Brocher (Hermance, Switzerland) to host the nursing AI workshop in November 2024.

opportunities.intro: Across all levels of collaboration and supervision, I encourage applicants from underrepresented groups in nursing research and academia to apply to work with me.
opportunities.collaboration_text: I welcome inquiries from health authorities, health systems stakeholders, NGOs, communities/community-organisations, and industry interested in health equity and health technologies to email me (healthinformatics.equity@ubc.ca) if you would like to work together.
opportunities.postdoc_text: Local and remote collaborations are welcomed. Please email me.
opportunities.grad_status: I am not accepting graduate students for the 2025 and 2026 academic intakes.
opportunities.grad_general: Applying to pursue graduate studies is not a decision to take lightly. Preparing a strong graduate application package takes time and initial relationship building with your potential supervisor. As a general guide, I do not entertain supervision inquiries made within 1 month of the application deadline.
opportunities.grad_interest: I am keen to mentor and support high-calibre thesis-stream Masters and PhD students who have a strong interest in research in the field of nursing and health informatics.

grad_programs.name: UBC Interdisciplinary Graduate Studies (IGS) Global Studies Stream
UBCO School of Nursing Graduate Programs

phd_requirements: You should have a foundational understanding of nursing/health informatics (courses or work experience)
Already obtained a Master’s degree in nursing, health informatics, or a related field
Published at least one peer-reviewed paper in a health informatics journal
opportunities.undergrad_text: I encourage UBCO Nursing students interested in completing a Y4 research preceptorship to contact me. I occasionally offer funded research internships, work study, or co-op positions.
application_requirements: A short description of the project you are interested in and how your background aligns with the project aims
Your background in health or nursing informatics, including supporting publications (required for PhD applicants)
Your CV and copy of a recent publication or other writing sample
Please put “[HIE!Lab]” in your subject line
